channel.title: Home on Alejandro Gamboa blog
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Alejandro Gamboa blog

item.title: Powerstatus
item.description:
Today, in Scripts to Work Less, I wanted to explore the idea of creating a small script to determine a machine’s startup, shutdown, and wake-up times.

In the case of Windows, I know this information can already be obtained using tools like Plaso and others. However, I don’t want to spend 30 minutes just to answer a very specific question. So, let’s get our hands dirty. The first step is to identify which system events are related to these activities.

item.title: Notepad Plus Plus
item.description:
This investigation started after an alert generated by an EDR: “Possible Masquerading Behavior”.

Possible Masquerading Behavior indicates that a process, file, or connection is impersonating another entity:

  • Imitates a name
  • Imitates a path
  • Imitates behavior
  • Attempts to hide its true identity

The detected process was:

C:\ProgramData\USOShared\svchost.exe -nostdlib -run C:\ProgramData\USOShared\conf.c

This process was launched by the following parent process:

“C:\Users\User\AppData\Roaming\Bluetooth\BluetoothService.exe” -k

Let’s analyze these processes in detail.

At first glance, the execution of svchost.exe from this location strongly indicates malware. We will analyze what this command does, but first we analyze the USOShared directory:

item.title: About
item.description:
Soy Diego Alejandro Gamboa, de preferencia Alejandro, entusiasta del hardware y el software, especialmente apasionado por desarmar y reconstruir cosas, totalmente convencido del metodo cientifico.

Mis principales campos de interes son:

  • Informatica forense
  • Redes
  • Programacion (C++, Python, C#, Rust)
  • Bases de datos (Este tema ya no tanto, es aburrido)

Quisiera compartir por este medio algunas de mis investigaciones o proyectos personales, que en ciertos momentos de trabajo me han facilitado la vida.
